Tsukuba → Nikko · One-Day Self-Drive

Nikko Road Trip

Nature, spirituality & history — a single unforgettable day across Tochigi's world-heritage highlands.

Specially planned for Barış Öktem & Mashuq Kurt

🚗 ~1h45m each way 📍 5 stops 🕛 08:30 – 17:30
Scroll ↓
The Route

Your Day on the Map

The red line traces the actual driving route from Tsukuba along the Kita-Kanto Expressway, up the hairpin Irohazaka road to Lake Chuzenji. Tap any marker or button to jump to a stop.

🧭 Open Turn-by-Turn Directions in Google Maps 🚗 174.5 km total · ~216 min driving
The Itinerary

Five Stops, One Day

From gold-leaf shrines to a 97-metre waterfall — each stop is timed to flow naturally into the next.

MorningSacred Gateway & World Heritage
1
10:15 AM · First Stop

Shinkyo Bridge

神橋 · The Sacred Bridge

The gateway to Nikko's shrines and temples — an iconic vermilion-red arched bridge spanning the Daiya River. One of Japan's three finest bridges and a picture-perfect way to begin the day.

World HeritagePhoto Spot15 min
2
10:00 AM · Second Stop

Nikkozan Rinnoji Temple

日光山輪王寺 · Founded 766 AD

Nikko's most important Buddhist temple. Step inside the grand Sanbutsudo Hall to stand before three towering, gold-lacquered statues of the mountain deities — a serene contrast to the shrines nearby.

Buddhist TempleSanbutsudo Hall1 hr
3
11:00 AM · Third Stop

Nikko Toshogu Shrine

日光東照宮 · Mausoleum of Tokugawa Ieyasu

The lavishly decorated Shinto mausoleum of shogun Tokugawa Ieyasu and a masterpiece of Edo-period craftsmanship. Look for the famous "three wise monkeys" and the sleeping-cat carvings among thousands of gilded details.

World HeritageEdo Architecture1.5 hr
AfternoonCultural Themes & Global Landmarks
Late AfternoonMajestic Nature
4
04:00 PM · Fourth Stop

Kegon Falls

華味の滝 · 97 m plunge

One of Japan's three most beautiful waterfalls, plunging a dramatic 97 metres from Lake Chuzenji. Ride the elevator down to the lower viewing deck for the most breathtaking head-on perspective of the cascade.

WaterfallViewing Elevator45 min
5
04:45 PM · Final Stop

Lake Chuzenji

中祭寺湖 · At the foot of Mt. Nantai

A tranquil highland lake formed by an ancient eruption of sacred Mount Nantai. End the day with a peaceful lakeside stroll and mountain reflections before beginning the scenic descent back toward Tsukuba.

Mountain LakeScenic StrollGolden hour
Before You Go

Travel Tips

🍽

Pork-Free Dining

Restaurants near Toshogu and Nikko station specialise in yuba (tofu skin) — a delicate local specialty and an excellent pork-free option for lunch.

🛣

The Irohazaka Road

The winding climb up to Kegon Falls and Lake Chuzenji is gorgeous but packed with hairpin turns. Drive slowly and enjoy the views — it's part of the experience.

⏱

Plan Your Timing

Allow roughly 1 hour 45 minutes each way between Tsukuba and Nikko via the Kita-Kanto Expressway. Depart by 08:30 to keep the full itinerary comfortable.

🍁

Best Seasons

Autumn (mid-October to early November) brings spectacular foliage around Lake Chuzenji and Kegon Falls, while spring and summer offer lush green highlands.

Tsukuba → Shinkyo Bridge → Rinnoji → Toshogu → Kegon Falls → Lake Chuzenji → Tsukuba
A one-day self-drive itinerary · Nikko, Tochigi Prefecture, Japan
